header.title: URL Encoder/Decoder — Encode & Decode URLs Online Free
header.description: Free online URL encoder and decoder to encode and decode URLs with special characters. Handle query parameters, URL components, and full URLs. Essential for web development and API testing — encode or decode instantly.
header.keywords: url encoder, url decoder, encode url, decode url, url encoding, url decoding, url encode online, percent encoding

encoding.pasteUrl: Paste a URL or text to encode…
encoding.pasteEncoded: Paste an encoded URL or string to decode…

howToUse.title: How to Use the URL Encoder/Decoder
howToUse.step1Desc: Select Encode to percent-encode a URL, or Decode to convert encoded text back to readable form.
howToUse.step2Desc: Choose URL Component to encode parts like query parameters, or Full URL for complete URLs.
howToUse.step3Desc: Paste the URL or text into the input area. The output updates automatically as you type.
howToUse.step4Title: Copy the encoded or decoded result
howToUse.step4Desc: Click Copy to copy the result to your clipboard, or use Swap to feed the output back as input.

items.question: What is URL encoding?
items.answer: URL encoding converts special characters into a percent-sign (%) followed by two hex digits so they can be safely transmitted in URLs.

items.question: When should I use URL Component vs Full URL?
items.answer: Use URL Component to encode only a query parameter value. Use Full URL to encode an entire URL safely.

items.question: Why does a space encode as + or %20?
items.answer: application/x-www-form-urlencoded encodes spaces as +, while URL percent-encoding uses %20. Both are valid depending on context.

items.question: Can I decode a URL that I encoded?
items.answer: Yes. Switch to Decode mode and paste the encoded string to get back the original text.
